title: Fractional Chief of Staff — Spec Solutions
summary: Built and optimised customer support operations for a startup; led international expansion, HR compliance, and EOR projects; provided leadership coaching and community engagement programmes.

title: Contributing Tech Writer & Content Strategist — Phandroid
summary: Spearheaded content strategy and execution for a leading Android/consumer tech publication, driving audience growth through feature articles, reviews, and SEO-optimised editorial.

title: Executive Assistant / Chief of Staff — Connection Engine
summary: Served as organisational glue for the Mission Circle — scheduling, project tracking in Coda, community beta support, and CRM/workflow administration across a 30/30/30/10 split.

title: Community Manager — The Nook
summary: Owner and operator of The Nook, a Discord community supporting Twitch streamers through their affiliate journey; helped over 10 streamers reach affiliate status across 100+ members.

title: Customer Support Hero (Technical) — Stamped
summary: Led technical support for enterprise clients at a reviews platform, resolving complex API and widget integration issues, partnering with dev teams, and improving resolution processes.

title: Editor & Operations Assistant — Can I Play That?
summary: Provided executive support to the CEO of a gaming accessibility platform — managing content pipeline, strategic partnerships, the 2019/2020 awards programme, Patreon, and audience growth (30% over two years).

title: Community Manager — Irish Tech Community
summary: Redesigned and scaled ITC's Slack community structure; founded the ITC Jobs Board (300% job posting growth, acquisition interest); grew Slack membership 250% in two years.

title: Developer Advocate — Samebug
summary: Bridged the product team and developer community — guidance on debugging and issue resolution, workshops and webinars, feedback collection, and direct platform support.

title: Project Manager & Research Assistant — Learnovate Centre, Trinity College Dublin
summary: Led the €3M EU-funded DEVELOP project across international partners — Agile methodologies, cross-border stakeholder communication, bottleneck removal, and onboarding/culture work.

title: Customer Support Specialist — CoderDojo
summary: Front-line support to CoderDojo community members via Zendesk — ticket categorisation, escalation, knowledge base contribution, trend analysis, and onboarding new dojo organisers.

title: Product Analyst — Donnerwood Media (Meez)
summary: Analysed consumer demographics and behaviour data using SQL — churn, LTV, retention metrics — to support data-driven decisions for Meez's freemium model; reported to senior stakeholders.

title: Chief of People (Operations & Communications) — Leaguepedia
summary: Supported CEO in scaling Leaguepedia from a small group to a 100+ member organisation — end-to-end hiring frameworks, SOPs, operational workflows, onboarding programmes, and cross-functional collaboration.
notes: Exact start/end months unknown; using Jan/Dec as approximations for year-only dates.

title: Customer Support Specialist — Vodafone
summary: Customer service across multiple South Dublin Vodafone stores — sales, technical troubleshooting, high satisfaction ratings, and training/mentoring new team members.

title: Diagnosing a Widget That Failed Four Different Ways
summary: Worked through a reviews widget that presented four different visible failure modes — reduced an ambiguous, frequently-escalated issue to a four-branch diagnostic without engineering involvement.

title: Turning a Gmail Inbox Into an Actual Support System
summary: Transformed a Gmail + unconfigured FreeScout inbox into a structured support operation; built a documentation-trained chatbot; eliminated a whole ticket category via self-serve status tracking.

title: Keeping a €3M Project Aligned Across Borders, Without Authority
summary: Kept a stalled EU-funded research project on track across international partner organisations using visibility rhythms and direct intervention — with no formal authority over any partner.

title: Error Log Interpreter
summary: Paste a browser console error or stack trace and get a plain-English breakdown with five structured sections — distinguishing where an error surfaces from where it originated.

title: Debug Helper
summary: A structured debugging coach for three modes: have an error (want a plan), code does the wrong thing, and total silence. Coaches process, not just answers; covers silent failure patterns explicitly.

title: PostHog CS Health Intelligence
summary: Research-backed prototype — structured JSON account model and Python script that produces a ranked morning action list for a CSM: URGENT / WATCH / OK, each with signal, reason, and suggested action.

title: PostHog CSM Copilot
summary: Customer Success copilot for a PostHog CSM role — paste any incoming signal, add customer context, and choose a mode: Triage + Next Action, Draft Response, Escalation Strategy, or Health Assessment.

title: FAQ Chatbot
summary: Paste a company FAQ or knowledge base and a customer message; Claude returns an answer grounded entirely in the docs — no vector database, no backend, no hallucination.

title: LoreBySpec Creator Tool
summary: Two-mode content assistant for LoreBySpec YouTube channel: Video Ideas (five titled angles with hooks from a topic) and Comment Reply (drafted reply in a warm lore-nerd voice).

title: PregVoice
summary: Layered accessibility project tackling one specific gap: blind and visually impaired people cannot independently read a pregnancy test. Works in phases from smartphone-first to hardware teardown research.
notes: Status is research/planning phase; no shipped code yet. Teardown (Clearblue MCU confirmed), patent landscape, and regulatory path researched.

title: Support Ticket Triage Tool
summary: Paste a customer message or error log; Claude returns a category, colour-coded urgency (Low to Critical), and a suggested first response ready to copy. Built on Haiku at ~0.2¢ per triage.

title: KH Lore Consistency Checker
summary: Theory stress-tester for Kingdom Hearts lore: paste a theory and relevant wiki links, get a structured AI analysis — contradictions, weaknesses, clarifying questions, and an overall verdict.

title: Date Proposal Website
summary: Recreation and technical deconstruction of a viral 'date proposal' website — running No button, four date-type paths (food/coffee/activity/cinema), live API discovery, and calendar invite generation.
notes: New tools added to vocabulary: foursquare-api, eventbrite-api, tmdb-api, icalendar.

title: Sponsorship Strategy for a TikTok Personality
summary: End-to-end sponsorship project for a TikTok creator in mental health and rehabilitation — sponsor research, outreach strategy, personalised outreach messages, and a full sponsorship document with audience metrics and packages.
notes: Retyped from 'project' to 'case-study' per Decision 1: consulting narrative output, not a built artifact.

title: Business Relocation & Expansion Strategy
summary: Supported a creative business expanding from US to Europe — HR compliance, EOR partner selection, local labour law navigation, and creative workspace scouting in a major European city.
notes: Retyped from 'project' to 'case-study' per Decision 1: consulting narrative output, not a built artifact.

title: Leadership Coaching for a Growing Team
summary: Provided coaching to a scaling startup's leadership team on conflict resolution, communication, and team alignment; implemented team-building and cross-functional goal alignment.
notes: Retyped from 'project' to 'case-study' per Decision 1: consulting narrative output, not a built artifact.

title: Optimising Customer Support Operations
summary: Partnered with a startup on ticket volume — implemented priority-based ticket allocation, knowledge base, and staff training; reduced response times 30% and increased CSAT.
notes: Retyped from 'project' to 'case-study' per Decision 1: consulting narrative output, not a built artifact.

title: Niche Market Strategy for Web Development
summary: Identified an underserved niche — custom webhooks and integrations for Twitch streamers — and helped a developer position, price, and market into it; client established a sustainable revenue stream.

title: Rebuilding a Denim Brand, Top to Tail
summary: An early-stage made-to-order denim brand in a trust hole, rebuilt from the customer experience inward.

lensByAudience.cs-support: Cut inbound at the source by fixing the cause, not the queue.
lensByAudience.implementation: Redesigned a customer-facing platform and built a deflection tool that changed how the system behaved.
lensByAudience.people-hr: Ran an EOR selection through to contract for a complex multi-jurisdiction team, and navigated cross-border duty-of-care.
lensByAudience.ops-strategy: A full turnaround: brand, build-vs-buy judgment, and the operational structure a scaling team needs.

notes: Dates approximate — engagement ran across 2024 but precise months unavailable. EOR selection reached contract with Oyster; expansion funding was pulled before operation at scale.

title: Technical Writing Portfolio
summary: Four reference-quality playbook documents across ops and HR systems, written for different audiences to show range: API troubleshooting, SQL data audit, EOR payroll explainer, and tiered escalation logic.
notes: Contains 4 sub-documents: API Troubleshooting Playbook (Zendesk→Slack), SQL Missing Data Audit Guide, EOR Payroll Processing Explainer, Support Escalation Playbook. Indexed as one item; deep-link anchors for sub-docs are within the page.

title: SaaS Integration Failure Diagnostic Guide
summary: Internal support enablement document for Tier 1 and 2 agents covering webhook delivery failures, API authentication breakdowns, and HRIS sync errors — structured to reduce unnecessary escalation.

title: Cat API Documentation
summary: Professional API reference documentation for The Cat API's images search endpoint — authentication, all query parameters, response schema, request/response examples, error states, and pagination.

title: Voice & Tone Guide
summary: Internal contributor guide defining voice, tone, and writing standards for technical documentation teams — voice characteristics, tone shifts by context, common mistakes, and a contributor checklist.

title: Voice Adaptation Showcase
summary: The same 2FA how-to guide written three times — in Apple, Mailchimp, and PostHog voice — with source guide citations, applied principles, and reflection on what was hardest to adapt.

title: Digital Spec — Portfolio Front Door
summary: The portfolio's branching front-door guide — tag-driven curation, built as an overlay, no AI.
